data.text: I’m reading the execution path and watchdog code first so I can tie the fix to the exact orchestration point, then I’ll wire the timeout into the post-commit review/land phase and add regression coverage.

data.text: I’ve confirmed the loop already has a bounded route-resolution path and a separate progress watchdog. Next I’m looking for the exact post-commit review/land call chain so I can add the timeout at the right layer without disturbing pre-route or phase-empty behavior.

I found the attempt watchdog wiring in the loop. The remaining work is to locate the post-commit orchestration boundary itself, because the timeout needs to sit around the review/land work, not around the earlier execution or liveness plumbing.

    22	// PostMergeReviewInput bundles the data RunPostMergeReview needs from its

    43	// reports whether the caller should treat this attempt as a success. When
    44	// the review path encountered a non-fatal Store error the caller is expected

    52	// harness/model/provider/profile pins from the original attempt.
    53	type PostMergeReviewOutput struct {
    54		Report        ExecuteBeadReport

    58		RepairContext *RepairContextFromReviewGroup
    59	}
    60	
    61	// RunPostMergeReview executes the post-merge review state machine for a
    62	// successful bead attempt. When review is skipped (no reviewer or --no-review)
    63	// the bead is closed and the function returns Approved=true. When review is
    64	// active, the work asks the review coordinator to evaluate the
    65	// close-eligible result before CloseWithEvidence runs; close only proceeds on
    66	// unanimous evidence-backed APPROVE. REQUEST_CHANGES and BLOCK record their
    67	// review events without reopening the bead.
    68	//
    69	// The review:skip label only skips when it carries a sibling
    70	// review:skip-reason:* label; otherwise it is ignored. When review is not
    71	// skipped, the review coordinator's verdict(s) or error drive the canonical
    72	// event emissions (review / review-error / review-manual-required /
    73	// review-malfunction), and on a non-APPROVE verdict the triage policy is
    74	// still consulted without reopening the bead.
    75	//
    76	// Returns Approved=true on the APPROVE path and on the skip path; returns
    77	// Approved=false on REQUEST_CHANGES, BLOCK, malformed APPROVE/BLOCK, or
    78	// reviewer transport error. Store errors are surfaced via StoreErrOp +

    86	func RunPostMergeReview(ctx context.Context, in PostMergeReviewInput) PostMergeReviewOutput {

   168				"attempt_id": report.AttemptID,
   169				"session_id": report.SessionID,
   170				"result_rev": report.ResultRev,
   171			},
   172		}
   173		reviewGroup, reviewErr := runPreCloseReviewGroup(ctx, in.Reviewer, in.Bead.ID, report.ResultRev, implRouting)
   174		reviewRes, reviewGroupErr := reducePreCloseReviewGroup(reviewGroup)
   175		chargeReviewCost := func() bool {
   176			budgetExceeded := false
   177			if capTracker := in.ReviewCostCap; capTracker != nil && reviewGroup != nil {
   178				deferred := false
   179				for _, slot := range reviewGroup.Slots {
   180					if slot.Result == nil {
   181						continue
   182					}
   183					capTracker.Add(slot.Result.ReviewerHarness, slot.Result.CostUSD)
   184					if detail, capped := capTracker.Tripped(); capped && !deferred {
   185						_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
   186							Kind:      "review-cost-deferred",
   187							Summary:   "review-cost-deferred",
   188							Body:      ReviewCostDeferredEventBody(report.ResultRev, slot.Result.CostUSD, capTracker.Spent(), capTracker.MaxUSD),
   189							Actor:     in.Assignee,
   190							Source:    "ddx work",
   191							CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
   192						})
   193						if in.Log != nil {
   194							_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(in.Log, "review cost cap deferred (%s %s): %s\n", in.Bead.ID, report.ResultRev, detail)
   195						}
   196						deferred = true
   197						budgetExceeded = true
   198					}
   199				}
   200			}
   201			return budgetExceeded
   202		}
   203		if reviewGroupErr != nil {
   204			reviewErr = reviewGroupErr
   205		}
   206		if reviewErr != nil {
   207			class := ClassifyReviewError(reviewErr, reviewRes)
   208			reviewerIndex, slotScoped := reviewErrorReviewerIndex(reviewGroup, reviewRes)
   209			prior := CountPriorReviewErrors(in.Store, in.Bead.ID, report.ResultRev)
   210			if slotScoped {
   211				prior = CountPriorReviewErrorsForSlot(in.Store, in.Bead.ID, report.ResultRev, reviewerIndex)
   212			}
   213			attemptCount := prior + 1
   214			maxRetries := in.Rcfg.ReviewMaxRetries()
   215			if maxRetries <= 0 {
   216				maxRetries = DefaultReviewMaxRetries
   217			}
   218			errSummary := EventBodySummary{
   219				InputBytes:  0,
   220				OutputBytes: 0,
   221			}
   222			if reviewRes != nil {
   223				errSummary = EventBodySummary{
   224					Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
   225					Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
   226					InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
   227					OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
   228					ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
   229				}
   230			}
   231			if attemptCount >= maxRetries {
   232				body := ReviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error())
   233				if slotScoped {
   234					body = ReviewErrorEventBodyForSlot(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewerIndex, reviewErr.Error())
   235				}
   236				body = AppendEventSummary(body, errSummary)
   237				_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
   238					Kind:      "review-manual-required",
   239					Summary:   class,
   240					Body:      body,
   241					Actor:     in.Assignee,
   242					Source:    "ddx work",
   243					CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
   244				})
   245				applyReviewOperatorRequiredParking(
   246					in.Store,
   247					in.Bead.ID,
   248					in.Assignee,
   249					now().UTC(),
   250					"review retry budget exhausted: "+class,
   251					"review error retry budget requires operator decision",
   252					"review the pre-close review failure and accept, retry, block, or cancel",
   253				)
   254			} else {
   255				body := ReviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error())
   256				if slotScoped {
   257					body = ReviewErrorEventBodyForSlot(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewerIndex, reviewErr.Error())
   258				}
   259				body = AppendEventSummary(body, errSummary)

   397	// Default configuration values.
   398	const (
   399		DefaultHarness = "codex"
   400		// DefaultTimeoutMS is the default idle (inactivity) timeout — resets on
   401		// every stdout/stderr byte or agent event. 2 hours is long enough for any
   402		// task where the provider streams progress; a stuck provider still needs
   403		// DefaultWallClockMS to guarantee termination.
   404		DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000 // 2 hours
   405		// DefaultWallClockMS is the absolute wall-clock cap applied in addition to
   406		// the idle timeout. It fires regardless of stream/event activity, so a
   407		// provider that emits heartbeats forever cannot pin a worker indefinitely.
   408		// Sized at 3h — roughly 1.5x the idle timeout — so normal long tasks still
   409		// complete while genuinely hung workers free themselves. See
   410		// RC2 of ddx-0a651925 for the incident that motivated this bound.
   411		DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000 // 3 hours
   412		// ToolCallTimeout is a per-tool-call sanity cap: if a single tool_call
   413		// event has no matching tool_result within this window, the drain cancels
   414		// the execution. This is NOT for loop detection (see loopDetector in
   415		// agent_runner_service.go) — it catches individually hung subprocesses
   416		// (e.g., network deadlock in git push) that would otherwise absorb
   417		// unlimited wall-clock without surfacing.
   418		ToolCallTimeout = 1800000 // 30 minutes
   419		DefaultLogDir   = ".ddx/agent-logs"
   420	)
   421	
   422	// ResolveLogDir returns an absolute session-log directory path anchored at
   423	// projectRoot. Callers that construct an agent.Runner must use this to avoid
   424	// the Runner's relative DefaultLogDir resolving against process CWD — which
   425	// historically wrote logs to stray locations like cli/internal/server/.ddx/.
   426	func ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, configured string) string {
   427		if configured == "" {
   428			configured = DefaultLogDir
   429		}
   430		if filepath.IsAbs(configured) {
   431			return configured
   432		}
   433		if projectRoot == "" {
   434			return configured
   435		}
   436		clean := filepath.Clean(filepath.FromSlash(configured))
   437		prefix := ddxroot.DirName + string(filepath.Separator)
   438		if clean == DefaultLogDir || clean == ddxroot.DirName || clean == "."+string(filepath.Separator)+ddxroot.DirName || clean == ".ddx" || clean == filepath.Clean(DefaultLogDir) || strings.HasPrefix(clean, prefix) {
   439			return projectStatePath(projectRoot, configured)
   440		}
   441		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, configured)
   442	}

    10	// CLIOverrides carries per-invocation flag values that override

    13	type CLIOverrides struct {

    26		// default.

    28		// ProviderRequestTimeout, when non-nil, overrides the per-request wall-clock
    29		// cap applied to a single Chat/ChatStream call. Corresponds to --request-timeout
    30		// on execute-bead and work. Zero pointer means "use config or model default".
    31		ProviderRequestTimeout *time.Duration
    32		// MinPower and MaxPower are passthrough power-bounds for the upstream agent
    33		// routing contract (CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-006). DDx passes these to
    34		// ServiceExecuteRequest unchanged; the agent owns model selection within the
    35		// bounds. Zero means no bound (unconstrained). Corresponds to --min-power and
    36		// --max-power on execute-bead and work.
    37		MinPower int
    38		MaxPower int
    39		// OpaquePassthrough, when true, prevents Resolve from falling back to
    40		// agent.model from the project config when the caller did not supply a
    41		// model explicitly. Used by the ddx work path (FEAT-010 / ddx-c4231775):
    42		// routing belongs to the agent service; DDx must not inject a
    43		// config-sourced model.
    44		OpaquePassthrough bool

    47	// Resolve produces a sealed ResolvedConfig by layering overrides onto
    48	// cfg. Overrides are applied as last-write-wins per field. The
    49	// returned ResolvedConfig does not alias cfg's storage — every map
    50	// and slice is deep-cloned via per-type Clone methods before being
    51	// captured.
    52	//
    53	// Resolve accepts a nil cfg and returns a ResolvedConfig populated
    54	// from package defaults, sealed normally.
    55	func (c *NewConfig) Resolve(overrides CLIOverrides) ResolvedConfig {
    56		r := ResolvedConfig{sealed: true}
    57	
    58		r.assignee = overrides.Assignee
    59		r.reviewMaxRetries = c.ResolveReviewMaxRetries()
    60	
    61		var agent *AgentConfig
    62		var workers *WorkersConfig
    63		if c != nil {
    64			agent = c.Agent.Clone()
    65			workers = c.Workers
    66		}
    67	
    68		r.noProgressCooldown = workers.ResolveNoProgressCooldown()
    69		r.noChangesVerificationTimeout = workers.ResolveNoChangesVerificationTimeout()
    70		r.maxNoChangesBeforeClose = workers.ResolveMaxNoChangesBeforeClose()
    71		r.heartbeatInterval = workers.ResolveHeartbeatInterval()
    72	
    73		r.harness = overrides.Harness
    74		r.explicitHarness = overrides.Harness != ""
    75	
    76		r.model = overrides.Model
    77		r.explicitModel = overrides.Model != ""
    78		if r.model == "" && !overrides.OpaquePassthrough && agent != nil {
    79			r.model = agent.Model
    80		}
    81	
    82		r.provider = overrides.Provider
    83		r.explicitProvider = overrides.Provider != ""
    84		r.profile = overrides.Profile
    85		r.effort = overrides.Effort
    86		r.minPower = overrides.MinPower
    87		r.maxPower = overrides.MaxPower
    88		r.passthrough = AgentPassthrough{
    89			Harness:  r.harness,
    90			Provider: r.provider,
    91			Model:    r.model,
    92		}
    93	
    94		r.permissions = overrides.Permissions
    95		if r.permissions == "" && agent != nil {
    96			r.permissions = agent.Permissions
    97		}
    98	
    99		if overrides.Timeout != nil {
   100			r.timeout = *overrides.Timeout
   101		} else if agent != nil && agent.TimeoutMS > 0 {
   102			r.timeout = time.Duration(agent.TimeoutMS) * time.Millisecond
   103		}
   104	
   105		if overrides.ProviderRequestTimeout != nil {
   106			r.providerRequestTimeout = *overrides.ProviderRequestTimeout
   107		}
   108	
   109		r.evidenceCaps = c.ResolveEvidenceCaps(r.harness)
   110		r.beadQualityLintBlockThresholdScore = c.ResolveBeadQualityLintBlockThresholdScore()
   111		r.beadQualityMode = c.ResolveBeadQualityMode()
   112	
   113		r.contextBudget = overrides.ContextBudget
   114		if r.contextBudget == "" && c != nil {
   115			r.contextBudget = c.EvidenceCaps.ResolveContextBudget()
   116		}
   117	
   118		if agent != nil {
   119			r.sessionLogDir = agent.SessionLogDir
   120			if agent.ReasoningLevels != nil {
   121				r.reasoningLevels = make(map[string][]string, len(agent.ReasoningLevels))
   122				for k, v := range agent.ReasoningLevels {
   123					r.reasoningLevels[k] = append([]string(nil), v...)
   124				}
   125			}
   126		}
   127		if c != nil && c.Executions != nil && c.Executions.Mirror != nil {
   128			r.mirrorConfig = c.Executions.Mirror.Clone()
   129		}
   130		if overrides.AttemptBackend != "" {
   131			r.attemptBackend = overrides.AttemptBackend
   132		} else if c != nil && c.Executions != nil {
   133			r.attemptBackend = c.Executions.AttemptBackend
   134		}
   135		if c != nil && c.Executions != nil && c.Executions.Docker != nil {
   136			r.executionsDocker = c.Executions.Docker.Clone()
   137		}
   138	
   139		r.triagePolicy = c.ResolveTriagePolicy()
   140		r.maxDecompositionDepth = c.ResolveMaxDecompositionDepth()
   141		r.acQualityMinScore = c.ResolveACQualityMinScore()
   142	
   143		return r
   144	}

   146	// LoadAndResolve is the canonical production entry point that produces
   147	// a sealed ResolvedConfig. It loads the project's .ddx/config.yaml from
   148	// projectRoot, layers overrides on top, and returns the immutable
   149	// ResolvedConfig.

   151	// On load error, LoadAndResolve still returns a usable, sealed

   106	// ResolveTriagePolicy returns the effective triage policy for this config,
   107	// layering any `triage.policies` entries on top of the binary default
   108	// ladders. Unknown failure-mode names and unknown action names are
   109	// silently dropped (schema validation surfaces typos at load time).
   110	func (c *NewConfig) ResolveTriagePolicy() triage.TriagePolicy {
   111		policy := triage.DefaultPolicy()
   112		if c == nil || c.Triage == nil || len(c.Triage.Policies) == 0 {
   113			return policy
   114		}
   115		for modeStr, actionStrs := range c.Triage.Policies {
   116			mode, err := triage.ParseFailureMode(modeStr)
   117			if err != nil {
   118				continue
   119			}
   120			ladder := make([]triage.Action, 0, len(actionStrs))
   121			for _, a := range actionStrs {
   122				act, err := triage.ParseAction(a)
   123				if err != nil {
   124					continue
   125				}
   126				ladder = append(ladder, act)
   127			}
   128			if len(ladder) == 0 {
   129				continue
   130			}
   131			policy.Ladders[mode] = ladder
   132		}
   133		return policy
   134	}

   140	// ResolveACQualityMinScore returns the effective AC quality minimum score.
   141	// Defaults to 0.5 when unset or out of range [0, 1].

   143		if c == nil || c.Intake == nil || c.Intake.ACQuality == nil || c.Intake.ACQuality.MinScore == nil {
   144			return defaultACQualityMinScore
   145		}
   146		s := *c.Intake.ACQuality.MinScore
   147		if s < 0 || s > 1 {
   148			return defaultACQualityMinScore
   149		}
   150		return s
   151	}

   157			return 0

data.text: I’ve narrowed the scope to one bounded helper: `RunPostMergeReview` is the blocking orchestration, so I’m going to add a worker-configured timeout around that call and surface a release + operator-attention path on deadline. I’m also adding config plumbing so the default is explicit rather than hard-coded in the loop.

     5	// TestLoopConfigOpts names every durable knob the execute-bead loop
     6	// reads via ResolvedConfig. Tests must specify each field explicitly;

    18	
    19	// TestTry_ReviewVerdict_ApproveRoundTrips verifies that when a ddx try execution
    20	// reaches post-merge review with an APPROVE verdict, the terminal verdict persists
    21	// in both the bead event stream and the .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl row.
    22	// This regression test ensures that review verdicts remain queryable for
    23	// cross-bead economics analysis (e.g., "what is the cheapest model that produces
    24	// approved work?") from the durable metrics.
    25	func TestTry_ReviewVerdict_ApproveRoundTrips(t *testing.T) {
    26		projectRoot := t.TempDir()
    27		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName))
    28		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
    29	
    30		targetBead := &bead.Bead{
    31			ID:         "ddx-try-review-approve",
    32			Title:      "Try bead with approval",
    33			Acceptance: "1. PASS_MARKER found in diff\n2. lefthook run pre-commit passes",
    34		}
    35		require.NoError(t, store.Create(targetBead))
    36	
    37		// Stub executor that returns success.
    38		executor := ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
    39			return ExecuteBeadReport{
    40				BeadID:     beadID,
    41				AttemptID:  "attempt-approve-001",
    42				Status:     ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
    43				ResultRev:  "result123abc",
    44				BaseRev:    "base123abc",
    45				SessionID:  "session-approve-001",
    46				Model:      "test-model",
    47				Harness:    "test-harness",
    48				Provider:   "test-provider",
    49				CostUSD:    1.5,
    50				DurationMS: 5000,
    51			}, nil
    52		})
    53	
    54		// Deterministic reviewer that returns APPROVE with per-AC evidence.
    55		reviewer := beadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, _ string, _ ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
    56			return &ReviewResult{
    57				Verdict:   VerdictApprove,
    58				Rationale: "All acceptance criteria met",
    59				PerAC: []ReviewAC{
    60					{Number: 1, Item: "TestFoo passes", Grade: "pass", Evidence: "TestFoo found in diff"},
    61					{Number: 2, Item: "lefthook run pre-commit passes", Grade: "pass", Evidence: "pre-commit gate green"},
    62				},
    63				RawOutput:       `{"verdict":"APPROVE","rationale":"All acceptance criteria met"}`,
    64				ReviewerHarness: "review-harness",
    65				ReviewerModel:   "review-model",
    66				InputBytes:      100,
    67				OutputBytes:     50,
    68				DurationMS:      1000,
    69			}, nil
    70		})
    71	
    72		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
    73			Store:    store,
    74			Executor: executor,
    75			Reviewer: reviewer,
    76		}
    77	
    78		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "test-worker"}
    79		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
    80	
    81		// AC 1: Run the worker once through the post-merge review flow.
    82		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
    83			Mode:            "once",
    84			WorkerID:        "test-worker",
    85			ProjectRoot:     projectRoot,
    86			SessionID:       "session-approve-001",
    87			EventSink:       io.Discard,
    88			PostMergeReview: true,
    89			FinalizeDurableAudit: func(report ExecuteBeadReport) error {
    90				return FinalizeDurableAttemptAudit(projectRoot, store, report)
    91			},
    92			NoReview: false,
    93		})
    94	
    95		require.NoError(t, err, "worker.Run must succeed")
    96		require.NotNil(t, result)
    97		require.Greater(t, len(result.Results), 0, "expected at least one result")
    98		report := result.Results[0]
    99	
   100		// AC 1: The attempt report must carry the APPROVE verdict.
   101		assert.Equal(t, string(VerdictApprove), report.ReviewVerdict,
   102			"report.ReviewVerdict must be APPROVE")
   103		assert.Equal(t, "All acceptance criteria met", report.ReviewRationale,
   104			"report.ReviewRationale must match the reviewer's rationale")
   105	
   106		// AC 2: The bead event stream must contain a review event with APPROVE verdict.
   107		events, err := store.Events(targetBead.ID)
   108		require.NoError(t, err, "store.Events must succeed")
   109	
   110		var reviewEvent *bead.BeadEvent
   111		for i := range events {
   112			if events[i].Kind == "review" && events[i].Summary == "APPROVE" {
   113				reviewEvent = &events[i]
   114				break
   115			}
   116		}
   117		require.NotNil(t, reviewEvent, "expected review event with APPROVE summary in event stream")
   118	
   119		// AC 1, AC 2: The .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl must contain the APPROVE verdict.
   120		metricsPath := attemptmetrics.AttemptsPath(projectRoot)
   121		f, err := os.Open(metricsPath)
   122		require.NoError(t, err, "metrics file must exist and be readable")
   123		defer f.Close()
   124	
   125		var metricsRow attemptmetrics.AttemptRow
   126		decoder := json.NewDecoder(f)
   127		for decoder.More() {
   128			err := decoder.Decode(&metricsRow)
   129			require.NoError(t, err, "metrics row must be valid JSON")
   130			if metricsRow.AttemptID == "attempt-approve-001" {
   131				break
   132			}
   133		}
   134	
   135		assert.Equal(t, "attempt-approve-001", metricsRow.AttemptID,
   136			"metrics row must have matching attempt_id")
   137		assert.Equal(t, string(VerdictApprove), metricsRow.ReviewVerdict,
   138			"metrics row.ReviewVerdict must be APPROVE")
   139		assert.Equal(t, "test-model", metricsRow.Model,
   140			"metrics row must preserve model from report")
   141		assert.Equal(t, "test-harness", metricsRow.Harness,
   142			"metrics row must preserve harness from report")
   143		assert.Equal(t, 1.5, metricsRow.CostUSD,
   144			"metrics row must preserve cost from report")
   145	}

   152	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
   153		mode := r.Mode
   154		if mode == "" {
   155			if r.Once {
   156				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
   157			} else {
   158				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
   159			}
   160		}

160		}
   161		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
   162		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
   163			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
   164		}
   165		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
   166			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
   167		}
   168		return mode, idleInterval
   169	}
   170	
   171	// DefaultRouteResolutionTimeout bounds routing preflight and the resolveRoute
   172	// viability check when ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.RouteResolutionTimeout is unset.
   173	// A hung resolver previously wedged the single-threaded worker for hours while
   174	// holding a lease (ddx-8f2e0ebf); 60s is short enough to release promptly and
   175	// long enough not to false-trip a slow-but-healthy resolver.
   176	const DefaultRouteResolutionTimeout = 60 * time.Second
   177	
   178	// effectiveRouteResolutionTimeout returns the configured route-resolution
   179	// timeout, falling back to DefaultRouteResolutionTimeout when unset.
   180	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) effectiveRouteResolutionTimeout() time.Duration {
   181		if r.RouteResolutionTimeout > 0 {
   182			return r.RouteResolutionTimeout
   183		}
   184		return DefaultRouteResolutionTimeout
   185	}
   186

   188	// same bead that trips the consecutive-wedge guard when
   189	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.ConsecutiveWedgeThreshold is unset. A single transient
   190	// wedge (count 1) stays below the default so the bead remains re-claimable; two
   191	// consecutive wedges sideline it for operator attention (ddx-9714eaac).
   192	const DefaultConsecutiveWedgeThreshold = 2
   193	
   194	// effectiveConsecutiveWedgeThreshold returns the configured consecutive-wedge
   195	// threshold, falling back to DefaultConsecutiveWedgeThreshold when unset.
   196	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) effectiveConsecutiveWedgeThreshold() int {
   197		if r.ConsecutiveWedgeThreshold > 0 {
   198			return r.ConsecutiveWedgeThreshold
   199		}
   200		return DefaultConsecutiveWedgeThreshold
   201	}

    23	// beadStrictnessMode returns the strictness mode for review based on bead
    24	// labels. kind:doc and kind:mechanical → mechanical; kind:refactor and
    25	// kind:chore → behavior-light; default → strict.
    26	func beadStrictnessMode(labels []string) string {
    27		for _, l := range labels {
    28			switch l {
    29			case "kind:doc", "kind:mechanical":
    30				return StrictnessMechanical
    31			case "kind:refactor", "kind:chore":
    32				return StrictnessBehavior
    33			}
    34		}
    35		return StrictnessStrict
    36	}
    37	
    38	// strictnessModeInstructions returns the per-mode evidence requirement text
    39	// injected into the reviewer prompt.
    40	func strictnessModeInstructions(mode string) string {
    41		switch mode {
    42		case StrictnessMechanical:
    43			return "mechanical — file presence, renames, or symbol evidence only; no test-name or runtime evidence required."
    44		case StrictnessBehavior:
    45			return "behavior-light — build green plus file/symbol evidence suffices; test-name match required only when an AC explicitly names a Test* function."
    46		default:
    47			return "strict — each AC must be anchored to a named Test* function or a diff-touched symbol; file-only evidence is insufficient."
    48		}
    49	}
    50	
    51	// ReviewRequestClarificationEventKind is the event kind appended to a bead
    52	// when the reviewer returns REQUEST_CLARIFICATION.
    53	const ReviewRequestClarificationEventKind = "review-request-clarification"
    54	
    55	// ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind is the kind:review-pairing-degraded event
    56	// kind appended to the bead when the post-merge reviewer was routed to the
    57	// same provider as the implementer. R4 (different reviewer) is best-effort
    58	// emergent: bumping MinPower usually picks a different candidate, but the
    59	// guarantee is not absolute. The event surfaces the degradation so operators
    60	// can see when reviewer diversity collapsed.
    61	const ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind = "review-pairing-degraded"
    62	
    63	// ReviewerEscalatedEventKind is the kind:reviewer-escalated event kind
    64	// appended when the reviewer's MinPower is bumped above the baseline
    65	// impl.ActualPower+1 due to prior review-error or review-pairing-degraded
    66	// events for the same result_rev.
    67	const ReviewerEscalatedEventKind = "reviewer-escalated"
    68	
    69	// ReviewACOverrideEventKind is appended when the reviewer's per-AC grade
    70	// contradicts the ac-check.json mechanical result for the same AC item.
    71	// The event records the mismatch count and per-AC reasons for accuracy auditing.
    72	const ReviewACOverrideEventKind = "review-ac-override"
    73	
    74	// acCheckOutput is the minimal shape of .ddx/executions/<id>/ac-check.json
    75	// needed for AC-grade mismatch detection.
    76	type acCheckOutput struct {
    77		Items []struct {
    78			AC     int    `json:"ac"`
    79			Result string `json:"result"`
    80		} `json:"items"`
    81	}
    82	
    83	// countACGradeMismatches compares the reviewer's per-AC grades against
    84	// the mechanical results from ac-check.json. Returns the number of mismatches
    85	// (reviewer says pass where ac-check says fail, or vice versa) and a list
    86	// of per-AC reasons. Ignores needs_judgment and error mechanical results.
    87	func countACGradeMismatches(acCheckJSONStr string, perAC []ReviewAC) (int, []string) {
    88		if acCheckJSONStr == "" || len(perAC) == 0 {
    89			return 0, nil
    90		}
    91		var check acCheckOutput
    92		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(acCheckJSONStr), &check); err != nil {
    93			return 0, nil
    94		}
    95		mechByNum := make(map[int]string, len(check.Items))
    96		for _, item := range check.Items {
    97			mechByNum[item.AC] = strings.ToLower(item.Result)
    98		}
    99		count := 0
   100		var reasons []string
   101		for _, ac := range perAC {
   102			mech, ok := mechByNum[ac.Number]
   103			if !ok {
   104				continue
   105			}
   106			// Only compare definitive mechanical verdicts; skip needs_judgment/error.
   107			if mech != "pass" && mech != "fail" {
   108				continue
   109			}
   110			grade := strings.ToLower(ac.Grade)
   111			if (mech == "fail" && grade == "pass") || (mech == "pass" && grade == "fail") {
   112				count++
   113				reasons = append(reasons, fmt.Sprintf("ac%d: ac_check=%s reviewer=%s", ac.Number, mech, grade))
   114			}
   115		}
   116		return count, reasons
   117	}

1	package agent
     2	
     3	import "strings"
     4	
     5	// OperatorCancelReason marks a landing preserved because the operator
     6	// cancelled the attempt mid-flight via /api/beads/<id>/cancel. ADR-022
     7	// §Cancel SLA: the worker aborts at the next safe point (between LLM turns /
     8	// git ops) and emits a preserved_for_review result with this exact reason.
     9	const OperatorCancelReason = "operator_cancel"
    10	
    11	// PushFailedReasonPrefix is the canonical Reason prefix written by the
    12	// landing layer when `git push` rejects the new tip after a local merge or
    13	// fast-forward succeeded. ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus matches on this prefix
    14	// to map a merged-locally-but-not-pushed outcome to push_failed instead of
    15	// success, so the bead stays open.
    16	const PushFailedReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push failed:"
    17	
    18	// PushConflictReasonPrefix marks a more specific failure mode than
    19	// PushFailedReasonPrefix: the local push was rejected because the remote
    20	// advanced, the loop attempted an automatic pull/merge to recover, and the
    21	// merge produced a conflict the loop cannot resolve. The bead is parked for
    22	// human review with a short cooldown rather than a year-long park.
    23	const PushConflictReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push conflict:"
    24	
    25	// FailureMode constants classify *why* an execution did not land cleanly.
    26	// They are orthogonal to Outcome/Status: a single failed attempt carries one
    27	// outcome (e.g. preserved) and one failure mode (e.g. merge_conflict) so
    28	// measurement surfaces can aggregate failures by cause.
    29	const (
    30		FailureModeContextOverflow                = "context_overflow"
    31		FailureModeMergeConflict                  = "merge_conflict"
    32		FailureModeTestFailure                    = "test_failure"
    33		FailureModeBuildFailure                   = "build_failure"
    34		FailureModeTimeout                        = "timeout"
    35		FailureModeAuthError                      = "auth_error"
    36		FailureModeNoChanges                      = "no_changes"
    37		FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced             = "no_evidence_produced"
    38		FailureModeRatchetMiss                    = "ratchet_miss"
    39		FailureModeNoViableProvider               = "no_viable_provider"
    40		FailureModeProviderConnectivity           = "provider_connectivity"
    41		FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled            = "harness_not_installed"
    42		FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint = "blocked_by_passthrough_constraint"
    43		FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied          = "agent_power_unsatisfied"
    44		FailureModeLockContention                 = "lock_contention"
    45		FailureModeWorktreeLost                   = "worktree_lost"
    46		// FailureModeRouteResolutionTimeout classifies an attempt that was parked
    47		// because route resolution / routing preflight exceeded the configured
    48		// route-resolution timeout. The lease is released and the bead is flagged
    49		// for operator attention rather than auto-retried (ddx-d8970a7b).
    50		FailureModeRouteResolutionTimeout = "route_resolution_timeout"
    51		// FailureModeProgressWatchdog classifies an attempt the progress watchdog
    52		// terminated because phase-empty heartbeats (harness/model/route all empty)
    53		// persisted past the phase budget. The worker appeared fresh to the
    54		// liveness TTL while making no forward progress, so the lease is released
    55		// and the bead is flagged for operator attention (ddx-dc23f001, parent
    56		// ddx-8f2e0ebf criterion B).
    57		FailureModeProgressWatchdog = "progress_watchdog"
    58		// FailureModeConsecutiveWedge classifies a bead the consecutive-wedge guard
    59		// stopped re-claiming because it wedged (route_resolution_timeout or
    60		// progress_watchdog) on consecutive claims up to the threshold. The bead is
    61		// parked to proposed for operator attention so the single-threaded worker
    62		// keeps draining the rest of the queue instead of re-wedging the same bead
    63		// indefinitely (ddx-9714eaac, parent ddx-8f2e0ebf criterion E).
    64		FailureModeConsecutiveWedge = "consecutive_wedge"
    65		// FailureModeAttemptIntegrity classifies an attempt that produced commits
    66		// and a clean agent exit but failed DDx's structured post-agent integrity
    67		// validation (post-commit mutation, empty pre-commit gate evidence, or
    68		// uncommitted tracked changes after the implementation commit). It is
    69		// distinct from implementation/agent failures so operators can tell a DDx
    70		// validation rejection apart from a genuine task failure (ddx-725b65b4).
    71		FailureModeAttemptIntegrity = "attempt_integrity"
    72		FailureModeUnknown          = "unknown"
    73	)
    74	
    75	// ResourceExhaustedStopMessage is the operator-visible message emitted when
    76	// execution must stop because the host cannot safely continue draining the
    77	// queue.
    78	const ResourceExhaustedStopMessage = "resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop"
    79	
    80	// ClassifyFailureMode derives a failure_mode for a worker-level result from
    81	// its outcome, exit code, and error message. Returns "" when the outcome is
    82	// task_succeeded (success carries no failure mode). task_no_changes always
    83	// maps to FailureModeNoChanges regardless of error text.
    84	//
    85	// For task_failed (or non-zero exit code), the error message is scanned for
    86	// well-known substrings. The order matters: context/auth/timeout patterns
    87	// are checked before generic test/build patterns so a "test timed out"
    88	// classifies as timeout rather than test_failure. Anything unrecognised
    89	// maps to FailureModeUnknown so aggregates never hide a missed pattern.
    90	func ClassifyFailureMode(outcome string, exitCode int, errMsg string) string {
    91		switch outcome {
    92		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
    93			if exitCode == 0 {
    94				return ""
    95			}
    96			// Degenerate: success outcome with non-zero exit. Treat as unknown
    97			// failure rather than success so the aggregate flags the anomaly.
    98		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
    99			return FailureModeNoChanges
   100		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
   101			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
   102		}
   103	
   104		lower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)
   105		switch {
   106		case IsLockContentionError(errMsg):
   107			return FailureModeLockContention
   108		case IsWorktreeLostError(errMsg):
   109			return FailureModeWorktreeLost
   110		case containsAny(lower,
   111			"passthrough constraint unsatisfiable",
   112			"passthrough constraint:",
   113			"max_power is less than min_power",
   114			"max_power exceeds min_power",
   115			"must be less than max_power",
   116			"no viable routing candidate for pins",
   117			"harness cannot satisfy power constraint",
   118			"harness pin incompatible with power bounds",
   119			"model pin incompatible with power bounds",
   120			"provider pin incompatible with power bounds"):

data.text: I’ve got the shape now: add a worker-configured post-commit timeout, wrap the `RunPostMergeReview` call with a bounded helper, and treat deadline expiry as a wedge release instead of a normal review failure. I’m editing config plumbing and the loop/test path together so the new behavior is covered end to end.

225	// WorkersConfig controls the Add/Remove-worker affordances on the workers
   226	// overview. `default_spec` supplies sane defaults for one-click worker

   228	type WorkersConfig struct {
   229		DefaultSpec *WorkerDefaultSpec `yaml:"default_spec,omitempty" json:"default_spec,omitempty"`
   230		MaxCount    *int               `yaml:"max_count,omitempty" json:"max_count,omitempty"`
   231		// NoProgressCooldown bounds how long a bead may sit without a phase
   232		// transition before the work forces the worker to give up. Parsed
   233		// via time.ParseDuration (e.g. "6h"). Empty string uses the built-in
   234		// default (6h). See SD-024 §Config extensions §WorkersConfig.
   235		NoProgressCooldown string `yaml:"no_progress_cooldown,omitempty" json:"no_progress_cooldown,omitempty"`
   236		// NoChangesVerificationTimeout bounds a synchronous verification_command
   237		// emitted via no_changes_rationale.txt. Parsed via time.ParseDuration

   239		NoChangesVerificationTimeout string `yaml:"no_changes_verification_timeout,omitempty" json:"no_changes_verification_timeout,omitempty"`
   240		// MaxNoChangesBeforeClose caps the number of consecutive no_changes
   241		// attempts the work tolerates before closing the bead as
   242		// no-progress. Zero or unset uses the built-in default (3). Negative
   243		// values are treated as unset.
   244		MaxNoChangesBeforeClose *int `yaml:"max_no_changes_before_close,omitempty" json:"max_no_changes_before_close,omitempty"`
   245		// HeartbeatInterval is how often a claim owner refreshes its heartbeat
   246		// while holding a bead. Parsed via time.ParseDuration (e.g. "15s"). Empty
   247		// string uses the built-in default that matches bead.HeartbeatInterval
   248		// (30s today).
   249		HeartbeatInterval string `yaml:"heartbeat_interval,omitempty" json:"heartbeat_interval,omitempty"`
   250	}

   262	// ResolveNoProgressCooldown returns the effective no-progress cooldown for
   263	// this config. Defaults to 6h when unset or unparseable.
   264	func (w *WorkersConfig) ResolveNoProgressCooldown() time.Duration {
   265		if w == nil || w.NoProgressCooldown == "" {
   266			return defaultNoProgressCooldown
   267		}
   268		d, err := time.ParseDuration(w.NoProgressCooldown)
   269		if err != nil || d <= 0 {
   270			return defaultNoProgressCooldown
   271		}
   272		return d
   273	}

   278		if w == nil || w.NoChangesVerificationTimeout == "" {
   279			return defaultNoChangesVerifyTimeout
   280		}

   282		if err != nil || d <= 0 {
   283			return defaultNoChangesVerifyTimeout
   284		}
   285		return d
   286	}

   292			return defaultMaxNoChangesBeforeClose
   293		}
   294		return *w.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose
   295	}

    30	func TestReport_OutcomeReason_Persists_BesideDisrupted(t *testing.T) {
    31		report := ExecuteBeadReport{
    32			BeadID:                      "ddx-test",
    33			Status:                      ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges,
    34			Disrupted:                   true,
    35			DisruptionReason:            "transport_error",
    36			OutcomeReason:               "transport",
    37			PredictedPower:              82,
    38			PredictedSpeedTPS:           35.5,
    39			PredictedCostUSDPer1kTokens: 0.012345,
    40			PredictedCostSource:         "catalog",
    41		}
    42

data.text: I’m adding the regression coverage next. One test will hang inside the post-commit reviewer until the deadline and assert the lease is released with `post_commit_review_timeout`; the other will exercise the same path with an approval that finishes before the budget so the existing close path still works.

I caught a race in the bounded helper: if the callee returns just after the deadline, we still need to classify it as a timeout. I’m tightening that now so the deadline wins even when the goroutine finishes on the same tick.

I found one more gap: the explicit `--no-review` / review-skip path still had an unbounded `CloseWithEvidence` call. I’m folding both the reviewer path and the plain close path under the same deadline so the post-commit land step is covered either way.

data.text: I’m tightening the timeout exit path so `--once` doesn’t immediately re-claim the same bead after releasing the lease. In drain/watch mode it can keep moving; in once mode it should stop after surfacing the timeout.

The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for `git add`, `git commit`, or other staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 still allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-b2846610">     <title>execute-bead: post-commit review/land wedge unguarded — progress watchdog cannot fire once harness is set; bound review+land with a timeout</title>     <description> PROBLEM
ddx work/try attempts wedge AFTER the implementation subprocess commits its fix: the worker enters the post-commit review+land orchestration and hangs indefinitely with phase=running, harness set (e.g. claude/codex), model="" route="", last_activity_at advancing every 30s via heartbeat, but no review completion, no land, no new commit. The lease is held until the process is killed. Observed and MANUALLY SALVAGED ~5x on 2026-05-27 (beads ddx-606fe854, ddx-dc23f001, ddx-9714eaac, ddx-58a69bb0; longest single wedge 97 min). This is the top blocker to unattended `ddx work` drain.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/work/watchdog.go phaseEmpty(harness, model, route) returns true only when ALL THREE of harness, model, route are empty.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2177 liveness.SetAttempt(candidate.ID, provAttemptID, PhaseRunning, profile, harness, model, profile, 0) sets harness (non-empty) once at attempt start and NEVER passes route; no later call updates model/route on the liveness record.
- Therefore once an attempt is running, harness != "" so phaseEmpty() is permanently false, and RunProgressWatchdog (watchdog.go:102, wired at execute_bead_loop.go:2210) can NEVER fire for the running/post-commit phase it was designed to catch (ddx-8f2e0ebf criterion B). The route-resolution timeout (ddx-d8970a7b) only bounds the PRE-route window. So the post-commit review+land hang is unguarded by both mechanisms.

PROPOSED FIX (recommended: bound the post-commit phase, do not rely on field-emptiness)
Add a configurable timeout around the post-commit review+land orchestration in the attempt (mirroring the route-resolution timeout d8970a7b): when the implementation subprocess has returned/committed and the worker is in review or land, bound that work with a deadline (default e.g. 15m). On expiry: cancel the attempt context, release the lease, mark the attempt failed/preserved, and emit an operator-attention event (reuse flagWedgedForOperatorAttention). Field-emptiness (phaseEmpty) is the WRONG discriminator for a running attempt that has already done real work, so the fix must be progress/elapsed-based, not field-based.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the route-resolution timeout (ddx-d8970a7b) or the external-close watcher (ddx-dc23f001 RunExternalCloseWatcher).
- Do not change the resolving-phase all-empty detection (still valid before harness is assigned).
- Do not change harness/model routing.

CROSS-REF: follow-up to closed epic ddx-8f2e0ebf (criterion B was not actually covered by the landed progress watchdog).     </description>     <acceptance> 1. The post-commit review+land orchestration is bounded by a configurable timeout (default documented); on expiry the attempt context is cancelled, the lease is released, and an operator-attention event is emitted. Verified by an integration test (e.g. TestExecuteBead_PostCommitReviewLandTimeoutReleasesLease in cli/internal/agent) that injects a hanging review/land stub and asserts the lease is released within timeout+epsilon with an operator-attention record.
2. A review+land that completes within the budget is unaffected (regression): TestExecuteBead_PostCommitReviewLandWithinBudgetCompletes (or equivalent) passes.
3. The fix is elapsed/progress-based, not phaseEmpty-based: a running attempt with harness set and model/route empty that exceeds the budget is released (asserted by the test in AC1), proving the watchdog gap is closed.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.

package agent

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func newPostCommitTimeoutTestWorker(t *testing.T, reviewer BeadReviewer) (*ExecuteBeadWorker, *bead.Store, *bead.Bead) {
	t.Helper()

	store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
	require.NoError(t, store.Init())

	target := &bead.Bead{
		ID:         "ddx-post-commit-review",
		Title:      "Post-commit review timeout regression",
		Acceptance: "1. review closes the bead when it finishes within budget",
	}
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(target))

	executor := ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
		_ = ctx
		return ExecuteBeadReport{
			BeadID:    beadID,
			AttemptID: "attempt-post-commit",
			Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
			Detail:    "implementation commit landed",
			BaseRev:   "base-post-commit",
			ResultRev: "result-post-commit",
			SessionID: "session-post-commit",
			Harness:   "codex",
			Provider:  "openai",
			Model:     "gpt-5",
		}, nil
	})

	return &ExecuteBeadWorker{
		Store:    store,
		Executor: executor,
		Reviewer: reviewer,
	}, store, target
}

func TestExecuteBead_PostCommitReviewLandTimeoutReleasesLease(t *testing.T) {
	reviewer := beadReviewerFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
		_ = beadID
		_ = resultRev
		_ = impl
		<-ctx.Done()
		return nil, ctx.Err()
	})

	worker, store, target := newPostCommitTimeoutTestWorker(t, reviewer)
	cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{
		Assignee:                     "timeout-worker",
		PostCommitReviewTimeout:      20 * time.Millisecond,
		HeartbeatInterval:            5 * time.Millisecond,
		NoProgressCooldown:           time.Hour,
		NoChangesVerificationTimeout: time.Minute,
		MaxNoChangesBeforeClose:      3,
	}
	rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))

	start := time.Now()
	result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
		Once:            true,
		PostMergeReview: true,
	})
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, result)
	require.LessOrEqual(t, time.Since(start), 500*time.Millisecond, "timeout path must release promptly")

	got, err := store.Get(target.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status, "post-commit timeout must not close the bead")
	assert.Empty(t, got.Owner, "post-commit timeout must release the lease")

	events, err := store.Events(target.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var attention *bead.BeadEvent
	for i := range events {
		if events[i].Kind == "operator_attention" && events[i].Summary == FailureModePostCommitReviewTimeout {
			attention = &events[i]
			break
		}
	}
	require.NotNil(t, attention, "timeout path must emit operator_attention")
	assert.Contains(t, attention.Body, fmt.Sprintf(`"reason":"%s"`, FailureModePostCommitReviewTimeout))
	assert.Contains(t, attention.Body, `"timeout":"20ms"`)
}

func TestExecuteBead_PostCommitReviewLandWithinBudgetCompletes(t *testing.T) {
	reviewer := beadReviewerFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
		_ = ctx
		_ = beadID
		_ = resultRev
		_ = impl
		return &ReviewResult{
			Verdict:         VerdictApprove,
			Rationale:       "all good",
			RawOutput:       `{"verdict":"APPROVE","rationale":"all good"}`,
			ReviewerHarness: "review-harness",
			ReviewerModel:   "review-model",
			InputBytes:      42,
			OutputBytes:     13,
			DurationMS:      7,
		}, nil
	})

	worker, store, target := newPostCommitTimeoutTestWorker(t, reviewer)
	cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{
		Assignee:                "success-worker",
		PostCommitReviewTimeout: time.Second,
		HeartbeatInterval:       5 * time.Millisecond,
	}
	rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))

	result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
		Once:            true,
		PostMergeReview: true,
	})
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotNil(t, result)

	got, err := store.Get(target.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status, "completed review+land must close the bead")
	assert.Empty(t, got.Owner, "completed review+land must not leave a lease behind")

	events, err := store.Events(target.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	for i := range events {
		assert.NotEqual(t, "operator_attention", events[i].Kind, "within-budget completion must not emit operator_attention")
	}
}

The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for `git add`, `git commit`, or other staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 still allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. ddx work/try attempts wedge AFTER the implementation subprocess commits its fix: the worker enters the post-commit review+land orchestration and hangs indefinitely with phase=running, harness set (e.g. claude/codex), model="" route="", last_activity_at advancing every 30s via heartbeat, but no review completion, no land, no new commit. The lease is held until the process is killed. Observed and MANUALLY SALVAGED ~5x on 2026-05-27 (beads ddx-606fe854, ddx-dc23f001, ddx-9714eaac, ddx-58a69bb0; longest single wedge 97 min). This is the top blocker to unattended `ddx work` drain.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/work/watchdog.go phaseEmpty(harness, model, route) returns true only when ALL THREE of harness, model, route are empty.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2177 liveness.SetAttempt(candidate.ID, provAttemptID, PhaseRunning, profile, harness, model, profile, 0) sets harness (non-empty) once at attempt start and NEVER passes route; no later call updates model/route on the liveness record.
- Therefore once an attempt is running, harness != "" so phaseEmpty() is permanently false, and RunProgressWatchdog (watchdog.go:102, wired at execute_bead_loop.go:2210) can NEVER fire for the running/post-commit phase it was designed to catch (ddx-8f2e0ebf criterion B). The route-resolution timeout (ddx-d8970a7b) only bounds the PRE-route window. So the post-commit review+land hang is unguarded by both mechanisms.

PROPOSED FIX (recommended: bound the post-commit phase, do not rely on field-emptiness)
Add a configurable timeout around the post-commit review+land orchestration in the attempt (mirroring the route-resolution timeout d8970a7b): when the implementation subprocess has returned/committed and the worker is in review or land, bound that work with a deadline (default e.g. 15m). On expiry: cancel the attempt context, release the lease, mark the attempt failed/preserved, and emit an operator-attention event (reuse flagWedgedForOperatorAttention). Field-emptiness (phaseEmpty) is the WRONG discriminator for a running attempt that has already done real work, so the fix must be progress/elapsed-based, not field-based.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the route-resolution timeout (ddx-d8970a7b) or the external-close watcher (ddx-dc23f001 RunExternalCloseWatcher).
- Do not change the resolving-phase all-empty detection (still valid before harness is assigned).
- Do not change harness/model routing.

CROSS-REF: follow-up to closed epic ddx-8f2e0ebf (criterion B was not actually covered by the landed progress watchdog).     </description>     <acceptance> 1. The post-commit review+land orchestration is bounded by a configurable timeout (default documented); on expiry the attempt context is cancelled, the lease is released, and an operator-attention event is emitted. Verified by an integration test (e.g. TestExecuteBead_PostCommitReviewLandTimeoutReleasesLease in cli/internal/agent) that injects a hanging review/land stub and asserts the lease is released within timeout+epsilon with an operator-attention record.
2. A review+land that completes within the budget is unaffected (regression): TestExecuteBead_PostCommitReviewLandWithinBudgetCompletes (or equivalent) passes.
3. The fix is elapsed/progress-based, not phaseEmpty-based: a running attempt with harness set and model/route empty that exceeds the budget is released (asserted by the test in AC1), proving the watchdog gap is closed.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.

data.text: The package run exposed a few failures, but they aren’t obviously all from the new timeout path. I’m reading the affected tests now so I can separate “introduced by this change” from “preexisting suite noise” before deciding whether there’s a real code fix left.

The failing integration tests point to one specific regression: post-commit work is now inheriting parent cancellation too aggressively, so a later cancel can prevent the bead from closing even after the implementation commit has landed. I’m checking whether the codebase already uses `context.WithoutCancel`; if not, I’ll switch the bounded post-commit step to an uncancelled child plus timeout so closure still completes.

I found the likely regression point. I’m switching the bounded post-commit step to ignore parent cancellation while still honoring the new timeout, which preserves terminal close behavior after a successful implementation and keeps the timeout guard intact.
